@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent filtered opportunities by CreatedDate in April 2023 (query_db function-call-1234391615131376329) and then computed turnarounds without restricting to contracts closed in April (execute_python function-call-12760608320453411584), so it answered the wrong cohort and returned the wrong agent.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent computed averages only for April opportunities that had a directly linked Contract (and only for the two contracts it manually queried), ignoring opportunities without ContractID__c or alternative contract matching (e.g., via AccountId), then picked the minimum from this incomplete subset and returned "#005Wt000003NEa3IAG", as shown by the merge in execute_python on the small contract set from query_db.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent filtered opportunities by CreatedDate in April 2023 (see query_db selecting WHERE CreatedDate LIKE '2023-04-%') rather than selecting opportunities that closed in April 2023 based on Contract.CompanySignedDate, leading it to compute averages on the wrong cohort and return 005Wt000003NEa3IAG instead of the ground-truth 005Wt000003NDEBIA4.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After selecting April 2023 opportunities, the agent joined to contracts only via Opportunity.ContractID__c (function-call-13473625528513089074) and did an inner merge on ContractId (function-call-7650839436940020148), excluding valid closes linked by AccountId and thereby computing the minimum on a truncated set that yielded 005Wt000003NJgAIAW instead of the true fastest agent 005Wt000003NDEBIA4.
B. Failure modes encountered:
